ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The child's mother took the small bird and put it in the nest with its siblings. If the siblings reject the small bird, she will take it back.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yeɛ Yakob sele ye kyɛ, “Mesrɛ wɔ, bɔ wɔ dumaa kyire me.” Na Biaa ne buale kyɛ, “Nzu ati yeɛ ebisa me dumaa ɔ?” Na oyirale Yakob wɔ berɛ.
translated: Jacob said to his sons, “Gather around, and listen to me.” And the children asked, “Why do you want us to listen to you?” And they mocked Jacob in his presence.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yiti Nyameɛ bɔle sona kyɛ ye bɔbɔ ye sɛso, Nyameɛ sɛso yeɛ ɔbɔle ye ɔ; ɔbɔle bienzua ne brasua.
translated: God created the world and everything in it; God's creation is good and beautiful; it includes both animals and plants.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Ngaka mukoraa bɔ bɛ nwo te ne, fa nyii ne beleɛ nzoo nzoo; ngaka bɔ bɛ nwo nde ne, fa nyii ne beleɛ nyɔ nyɔ;
translated: When the old man is there, the child will not be afraid; when the old man is not there, the child will be very afraid.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔhane kyɛ, “Me Mene, sɛ alo me anwu me anwumvoin de a, nzaade mesrɛ wɔ, nnesã w’akoaa nwo kɔ.
translated: If you say, "I am the one, if I die and my children are left behind, they will be in distress, they will not have anyone to take care of them."

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The child's mother told the story of how she and her siblings were left alone in the forest. If the mother and siblings had not returned from the forest, they would have been left there.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yeɛ Yakob sele ye kyɛ, “Mesrɛ wɔ, bɔ wɔ dumaa kyire me.” Na Biaa ne buale kyɛ, “Nzu ati yeɛ ebisa me dumaa ɔ?” Na oyirale Yakob wɔ berɛ.
translated: Jacob said to him, "Please, tell me your name." But he said, "Why do you ask my name?" And he blessed Jacob there.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yiti Nyameɛ bɔle sona kyɛ ye bɔbɔ ye sɛso, Nyameɛ sɛso yeɛ ɔbɔle ye ɔ; ɔbɔle bienzua ne brasua.
translated: God created the moon and the stars, and God's stars are his children; the moon is his wife and the sun is his brother.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Ngaka mukoraa bɔ bɛ nwo te ne, fa nyii ne beleɛ nzoo nzoo; ngaka bɔ bɛ nwo nde ne, fa nyii ne beleɛ nyɔ nyɔ;
translated: If you are a child and you are crying, your mother will carry you on her back; if you are an adult and you are crying, your mother will carry you in her heart.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔhane kyɛ, “Me Mene, sɛ alo me anwu me anwumvoin de a, nzaade mesrɛ wɔ, nnesã w’akoaa nwo kɔ.
translated: If you don't want me, if you don't want me to die and my death to be in vain, then listen to me, don't let your children go.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The child who does not listen to advice will not grow well and will not succeed in life. If the advice is not followed, it will be difficult for them to achieve their goals.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.

translated: Jacob said to him, “Tell me your name.” But he replied, “Why do you ask my name?” Then he blessed Jacob there.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yiti Nyameɛ bɔle sona kyɛ ye bɔbɔ ye sɛso, Nyameɛ sɛso yeɛ ɔbɔle ye ɔ; ɔbɔle bienzua ne brasua.
translated: God created the moon and the stars, and God's stars are the moon; the moon has two sides and arms.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.

translated: When the old man is walking, he is like a snail; when the old woman is walking, she is like a tortoise.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔhane kyɛ, “Me Mene, sɛ alo me anwu me anwumvoin de a, nzaade mesrɛ wɔ, nnesã w’akoaa nwo kɔ.
translated: But he said, “My Lord, if I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass by your servant. Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest under the tree."

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The child's mother will not allow him to go to the farm with his friends because he has not completed his homework. If his friends finish their homework early, they can go without him.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yeɛ Yakob sele ye kyɛ, “Mesrɛ wɔ, bɔ wɔ dumaa kyire me.” Na Biaa ne buale kyɛ, “Nzu ati yeɛ ebisa me dumaa ɔ?” Na oyirale Yakob wɔ berɛ.
translated: Jacob said to them, “My brothers, come and buy from me.” But they said, “Why should we buy from you?” And they despised Jacob at that time.
